codeReview.summary: Clean, well-structured implementation that fully meets all 8 requirements. The MEV toggle (HTML, CSS, JS) was completely removed and replaced with an info button + modal dialog. Chain-specific content renders correctly for all specified chains (Ethereum/Flashbots, BSC/bloXroute, L2 sequencer, Polygon/Avalanche no-RPC). Modal is properly dismissible (X, Escape, overlay click). Accessibility is solid with role=dialog, aria-modal, aria-labelledby, aria-haspopup, and focus management. Add to Wallet buttons correctly disable without wallet connection. URL param cleanup for stale mevProtection params is handled both in updateUrlFromCompareParams and on page init. The executeCardTransaction function was simplified by removing the MEV protection options parameter. All 91 tests pass, typecheck clean, lint clean (only 2 pre-existing complexity warnings).

issues.description: renderMevChainContent() uses innerHTML to inject chain-specific HTML and then attaches click listeners to the newly created buttons by ID. This is safe since the HTML is hardcoded (no user input), but if the modal is opened, chain changed while modal is open, the content re-renders correctly. Minor: the event listener attachment after innerHTML is a pattern that works but could be fragile if the HTML structure changes. Consider event delegation on the mevChainContent container in the future.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly identified this as a client-side-only change and skipped the Red Phase (test-first) step per the skill's guidance that 'client-side-only changes (inline JS)' are tested via manual verification. The skill procedure was followed correctly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows typecheck/lint/test run before implementation, then implementation, then verification with typecheck/lint/test again plus 7 interactive browser checks.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mev-protection.md library file was created/updated to document the chain-specific MEV guidance and wallet_addEthereumChain params. This is good shared state that future workers can reference.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/mev-protection.md contains accurate chain table, RPC params, and modal text matching the implementation.

summary: Feature passes review. All 8 requirements are fully implemented: MEV toggle removed, info button with shield icon added, modal with MEV explanation and Flashbots docs link, chain-specific content for Ethereum/BSC/L2/Polygon/Avalanche, modal dismissible via X/Escape/overlay click, brutalist design, Add to Wallet disabled without wallet, URL param persistence removed. Code quality is good with proper accessibility attributes and focus management. No blocking issues found.
